h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: So 3/8 is three eighth-note beats, not a fourth count.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue applying the two-box distinction correctly and consolidate the separation of counting and beat-unit functions.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [sets three tiles in the next bar, then swaps in the eighth-note card over the lower box] 'Three tiles to this bar line, so the top label stays 3. With this card in the lower box, the lower label changes to 8. No fourth tile gets added.'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently performs the card-swap operation on a new bar, correctly deriving the changed lower number and confirming no extra tile appears, enacting the two-box criterion rather than merely restating it.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Top number, bottom number: one bar, one beat unit. Keep the tiles fixed: the card changes the lower number and the beat mark drawn under each tile; it adds no fourth beat. Three tiles, three eighth-note beats. Take that count into the entry.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: L4 raises a genuine new question about whether the card governs only the lower number or also the drawn note, but T4 answers it by restating the same two-box formula and confirming both functions without introducing a changed device, role, or evidence standard, so neither the stock-taking contrast nor a new public mechanism is legible on the page.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Top number, bottom number: one bar, one beat unit. Keep the tiles fixed: the card changes the lower number and the beat mark drawn under each tile; it adds no fourth beat.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The tutor's response to L4's precise question is a restatement of the already-established formula rather than a fitted new device, so it lacks specificity, route novelty, and learner agency relative to the actual blockage.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: Because no pivot learner turn exists, there is no later tutor uptake to a revised learner frame to score.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to continue naming the acting object and placing the corresponding card for each remaining arrow in sequence.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Earth on cart: down. The up arrow is track on cart. Track card goes there; the track pushes up on the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner independently applies the 'other object first, then direction' criterion to two arrows in sequence without tutor prompting for the second, demonstrating the procedure has moved from scaffolded to self-directed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or false closure that creates pressure for a route change; the tutor continues the same established object-first labeling sequence throughout.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is present because no reversal trigger occurs and the tutor does not change route, device, or evidence standard.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible or present.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: Earth on cart: down. The up arrow is track on cart. Track card goes there; the track pushes up on the cart.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner independently applies the 'other object first, then direction' criterion to two arrows in sequence without tutor prompting for the second, demonstrating the procedure has moved from scaffolded to self-directed.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible or present.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or false closure that creates pressure for a route change; the tutor continues the same established object-first labeling sequence throughout.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: There is no learner resistance, breakdown, or false closure that creates pressure for a route change; the tutor continues the same established object-first labeling sequence throughout.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is present because no reversal trigger occurs and the tutor does not change route, device, or evidence standard.

scored.recoheredEarlier: I can copy the pattern, but I am not sure I am using it instead of just memorizing the words.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: the row limit is the check
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was consolidating the row-limit formula and applying it to new cells, heading toward smooth procedural transfer.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: `42` goes in the one recorded amount box for that city-month row, I think. It is not an average, and it is not the header making a claim about every row.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L3 performs the three-way classification task the tutor set, which is a new device beyond mere acceptance, but the performance is tentative and closely mirrors the tutor's own framing, limiting its independence.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Next cell: write `month` the same way, then test `April`: does the note keep it as this row's month, or let `month` drift into the whole column?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 continues the same route — apply the row-limit pattern to a new cell — without any stock-taking contrast acknowledging that the pattern-application might be rote, and introduces no new device or representation; the adaptive mechanism required by L4's pressure is absent because T3 precedes L4 and no tutor turn follows L4 in the transcript.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No tutor turn appears after the reversal-trigger L4, so no adaptive mechanism can be evaluated.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no tutor turn after the pivot L4 in the transcript, so no recognition-contingent uptake is possible.
